scenario.title: Issue enrichment should identify useful build, borrow, buy options

metrics.label: Build-vs-buy leverage
metrics.denominator: Issue handoff should reduce wasted implementation effort by finding credible external precedent.
metrics.dataSource: issue enrichment packet and research links
metrics.scoringRule: 4 means at least one directly relevant repo/library precedent is cited with a usable implementation wedge.

metrics.label: Vision and product alignment
metrics.denominator: Enrichment should connect work to repo vision, non-goals, and user value.
metrics.scoringRule: 3 means product fit and non-goals are stated but not yet validated by user feedback.

metrics.label: Implementation readiness
metrics.denominator: Output should become an agent-start packet with acceptance criteria and proof plan.
metrics.scoringRule: 4 means acceptance criteria, proof plan, and first wedge are clear.

metrics.label: Downstream agent enablement
metrics.denominator: A follow-up agent should know what to do without rereading the whole thread.
metrics.scoringRule: 4 means clear next action, constraints, and evidence links.

metrics.label: Cost and throttle control
metrics.denominator: Research mode should be triggered and capped, not backlog-wide.
metrics.scoringRule: 4 means separate allowlist, repo throttles, and no old-backlog processing by default.

metrics.label: Lifecycle and routing clarity
metrics.denominator: Issue enrichment should make owner, route, and follow-up status visible.
metrics.scoringRule: 3 means routing is visible but not yet integrated with a broader product roadmap lane.

hardGates.name: old_backlog_scan
hardGates.detail: Fixture assumes triggered enrichment only, not backlog-wide scanning.
